name: Percentage of flexitarians - Group: All adults
descriptionShort: Percentage of participants who responded to the question "Which of these best describes your diet?" with "Flexitarian: mainly vegetarian, but occasionally eat meat or fish".
datasetName: Dietary choices of Brits and Americans

origins.title: Dietary choices of Brits
origins.description:
This dataset contains the result of semiannual YouGov surveys of around 2,000 adults aged 18 years and older in the UK, asking the question: "Which of these best describes your diet?". The available responses were:
- Meat eater: eat meat and/or poultry.
- Flexitarian: mainly vegetarian, but occasionally eat meat or fish.
- Pescetarian: eat fish but do not eat meat or poultry.
- Vegetarian: do not eat any meat, poultry, game, fish, or shellfish.
- Plant-based / Vegan: do not eat dairy products, eggs, or any other animal product.
- None of these.
origins.citationFull: YouGov (2026) - Dietary choices of Brits (e.g. vegetarian, flexitarian, meat-eater etc)?

origins.title: Dietary choices of Americans
origins.description:
This dataset contains the result of semiannual YouGov surveys of around 1,000 adults aged 18 years and older in the US, asking the question: "Which of these best describes your diet?". The available responses were:
- Meat eater: eat meat and/or poultry.
- Flexitarian: mainly vegetarian, but occasionally eat meat or fish.
- Pescetarian: eat fish but do not eat meat or poultry.
- Vegetarian: do not eat any meat, poultry, game, fish, or shellfish.
- Plant-based / Vegan: do not eat dairy products, eggs, or any other animal product.
- None of these.
origins.citationFull: YouGov (2026) - Dietary choices of Americans (e.g. vegetarian, flexitarian, meat-eater etc)?
